population: Adult patients with refractory septic shock requiring norepinephrine >0.5 mcg/kg/min to maintain mean arterial pressure
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: SOFA score was the only significant independent predictor in multivariate analysis. Age, P/F ratio, lactate concentration, procalcitonin level, and norepinephrine dose did not show predictive ability in multivariate analysis. SOFA range 8-16 points.

population: Adult patients with refractory septic shock requiring norepinephrine >0.5 mcg/kg/min to maintain mean arterial pressure
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: Norepinephrine dose was identified as a significant predictor in ROC analysis alongside SOFA score, despite not reaching significance in multivariate logistic regression.

population: Patients with sepsis who developed refractory septic shock, aged 42–74 years (mean 59.7 ± 7.8), SOFA score 8–16 points, requiring norepinephrine ≥ 0.5 µg·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹ to maintain MAP > 65 mmHg, on mechanical ventilation ≤ 24 h at inclusion
timing: Within 24 hours of septic shock diagnosis
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: Sepsis diagnosed per Sepsis-3 criteria. Refractory septic shock defined as requiring norepinephrine > 0.5 µg·kg⁻¹·min⁻¹ to maintain MAP > 65 mmHg. No primary outcome data reported in this excerpt.

population: Patients with septic shock managed in ICU according to Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines
effect_size: 44% (35/80 patients) 28-day mortality; 17% (6 patients) of fatal outcomes within first 3 days
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: Primary infection source: lungs 55%, abdominal cavity 35%, unknown/other 10%. Causes of death: cardiovascular failure progression 85% (30 patients), DIC 11% (4 patients), respiratory failure/ARDS progression 3% (1 patient).

method: Multivariate regression analysis and ROC analysis
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: SOFA score was the only significant independent predictor of lethal outcome in refractory septic shock. Other candidate predictors (P/F ratio, need for renal replacement therapy, lactate, norepinephrine dose) did not demonstrate significance in multivariate analysis.

method: ROC analysis and logistic regression analysis
mechanical_ventilation: Required mechanical ventilation ≤24 h at inclusion
notes: Multivariate analysis did not confirm high prognostic significance of several indicators that appeared significant in univariate analysis. Authors note that univariate analysis alone is insufficient and multivariate analysis is necessary to properly identify predictors of adverse outcome. Possible limitation noted as insufficient sample size.
